The '''modular taskforce cruiser''', also known as the '''Imperial star hauler''', was a support [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] class built by [[Tagge Industries Shipyards Limited]] for the [[Dark Empire]]. These ships functioned as a generic framework on which [[Mission Function Module]]s could be mounted and reconfigured as necessary. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], the modular taskforce cruiser [[Reliance (star hauler)|''Reliance'']] was infiltrated by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] to [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|strike]] at the [[Emperor's Citadel|citadel]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Swgm8c2.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The cruiser in fleet formation, seen from the bow.]]
The basic design of the modular taskforce [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] was fairly simple. In its baseline configuration, the ship consisted of a command section, a life-support system, quarters for a skeleton crew, and an engineering section. Since this was a support vessel, armament was light for its size. On its own, the baseline configuration did little more than transport a small amount of cargo. The real purpose of this framework was to carry mission-specific modules.<ref name="DESB" />

==Mission modules==
{{Main|Mission Function Module}}
[[Mission Function Module]]s, also known as "MFMs," were independent units which were attached to the skeleton of the modular taskforce cruiser. Each vessel carried one module, but it was believed that Tagge planned to produce miniature modules to allow a ship to perform several simultaneous functions. In a single day at a [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] or other properly outfitted facility, one module could be removed, and a new module could be mounted and connected to the vessel's life support, power, and communications systems.<ref name="DESB" />

===Hospital module===
{{Main|Hospital module}}
Outfitted with [[Metric ton/Legends|tons]] of medical equipment and staffed with thousands of [[Medical droid/Legends|medical droids]] and personnel, the hospital module was used to assist in numerous types of disaster situations. It cost 750,000 credits.<ref name="DESB" />

===Inquisition module===
{{Main|Inquisition module}}
Used mainly to punish rebellious planets, the inquisition module was outfitted with equipment for mass executions, destroying a planet's information infrastructure, reinforcing the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] presence on a world, or completely destroying the world's population if necessary. It cost 425,000 credits.<ref name="DESB" />

===Observation module===
{{Main|Observation module}}
Designed for long-range espionage, this module carried 500 [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] among its cargo, and was employed in observation actions from outside the border of enemy territory. It cost 6.3 million credits. It carried 12 [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] for defense.<ref name="DESB" />

===Rescue module===
{{Main|Rescue module}}
The rescue module was used to assist in the aftermath of a space battle. The 40,000 metric tons of cargo capacity were mainly dedicated to repair parts for starships, and the crew included 2,130 technicians. The 140 doctors aboard would search for any survivors remaining in the area, and the technicians would make whatever field repairs were possible to fly damaged ships to the nearest friendly shipyard. Ships with this module were intended to be paired with a hospital ship. It cost 500,000 credits.<ref name="DESB" />

===Survey module===
{{Main|Survey module}}
The survey module was designed for rapid planetary and system exploration operations. Assisted by its complement of [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] and [[Droid/Legends|droids]], it was capable of completely analyzing a planet, including surveying for natural resources and good colony sites, within a standard day. It could also plot viable [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] from the survey location to major trade routes. It cost one million credits and contained over 100 airspeeders, 5 [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|Skipray blastboats]], and 6 [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] for defense.<ref name="DESB" />

==History==
[[File:ModularTaskforceCruiser-DarkEmpire1.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A Modular Taskforce Cruiser over [[Byss/Legends|Byss]].]]
Though the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] had long preferred to maintain a fleet of ships which each filled a specific niche, this was an expensive policy which became progressively harder to maintain. After a report was commissioned on streamlining fleet operations, [[Tagge Industries Shipyards Limited]] won the contract for a new multi-role [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. The modular taskforce cruiser was the result of a design program which recognized the fundamental similarity of many single-role designs.<ref name="DESB" />

In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], during the [[Dark Empire|reign]] of the [[Cloning/Legends|reborn]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> at least one of the cruisers was stationed over the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]],<ref name="DE2">[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]]</ref>
and was later in use by the Imperial forces under [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="DE4">[[Dark Empire 4|''Dark Empire'' 4]]</ref> The modular taskforce cruiser<ref name="DESB" /> [[Reliance (star hauler)|''Reliance'']] was employed as a star hauler, ferrying cargo into the [[Deep Core Security Zone/Legends|Byss Security Zone]]. The ''Reliance'' was infiltrated by<ref name="DEII4" /> [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]<ref name="NEC" /> [[Commando/Legends|commandos]], who stowed away inside a shipment of [[X-1 Viper|Viper Automadon]] [[Battle droid/Legends|war droids]] carried in its cargo hold. Carried to port by the star hauler and [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttled]] down to Byss, the commandos hijacked the war droids and launched an [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|assault]] on the [[Emperor's Citadel]].<ref name="DEII4" />

==Behind the scenes==
The modular taskforce cruiser<ref name="DESB" /> first appeared in the [[Dark Empire 2|second issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="DE2" /> on [[February 18]]th, [[1992]].<ref name="Marvel1">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54058/star_wars_dark_empire_1991_2|text=Star Wars: Dark Empire (1991) #2}}</ref> The ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', written by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and released on [[June 15]]th, [[1993]]<ref name="BN">{{BN|url=w/star-wars-the-roleplaying-game-west-end-games/1018175933|text=Star Wars the Roleplaying Game: Dark Empire Sourcebook}}</ref> gave the starship the name "modular taskforce cruiser,"<ref name="DESB" /> while it was identified as an "Imperial star hauler" in the [[Dark Empire II 4|fourth issue]] of ''Dark Empire''{{'s}} [[Star Wars: Dark Empire II|sequel series]], likewise created by Veitch and Kennedy,<ref name="DEII4" /> and published on [[March 21]]st of [[1995]].<ref name="Marvel2">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54066/star_wars_dark_empire_ii_1994_4|text=Star Wars: Dark Empire II (1994) #4}}</ref>
